behaviors.description: Content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is in the data-collection orchestrator's handoff to the summary step — whether all three collectors are actually invoked and their results assembled into a single payload. A unit test of any one collector misses whether the aggregation wires them all together. No real HTTP or LLM needed; stub the three clients and observe the argument passed downstream.
behaviors.test_description: Stub all three data-source clients (metrics endpoint, issue tracker, deployment log) to return fixture responses. Wire them through the real collection-and-aggregation layer. Intercept the call to the summary-generation function and capture its input argument. Assert the argument contains non-empty, correctly labeled fields from each of the three sources.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode — swallowing an error from one collector while allowing the rest of the pipeline to continue and emit a partial report — only manifests when the error-handling code interacts with the aggregation and report-formatting layers. A unit test of the error handler alone cannot prove the pipeline does not abort. No real HTTP or LLM needed; stub one client to raise an exception.
behaviors.test_description: Stub one of the three data-source clients to raise a connection error; let the other two return fixture data. Run the full collection-aggregation-formatting pipeline. Assert the resulting report object contains a `data unavailable` marker in the failing source's section, contains real data in the other two sections, and is returned rather than raising an exception.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: "Accurately represents" is a quality judgment on generated text. If the summary is produced by an LLM, the model may faithfully reflect values on one run and omit or hallucinate them on another — outcomes vary across calls. Mocking the LLM removes the only thing being tested. A deterministic template renderer would lower this to Integration, but a generative model makes correctness non-deterministic and therefore Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the real summary-generation function — backed by the live LLM API — a structured payload containing specific key metrics (e.g., deployment count=12, open critical issues=5, error rate=0.03%). Assert the returned narrative text references each of those specific values accurately and does not introduce fabricated figures. Run multiple times to surface non-deterministic failures.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This is the full pipeline: real HTTP calls to three data sources → real LLM summary generation → real SMTP/email delivery → inbox receipt. It requires at least one agentic step (LLM summary) plus real external infrastructure (email). A failure can occur at any seam, and mocking any major component hides a real integration gap. MULTI-STEP applies: data collection, agentic summarization, and delivery are sequential steps spanning real systems.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full pipeline end-to-end in a staging environment: point all three data-source URLs at live or sandboxed test instances, call the real LLM summary API, and send via the real SMTP relay to a dedicated test inbox. Assert the test inbox receives exactly one new email within a timeout, that the subject is non-empty, that the body contains metric values, and that all three source sections are present.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: The behavior spans two pipeline runs and real filesystem I/O: (1) email client raises a connection error → report is persisted to disk; (2) pipeline is invoked again → persisted file is picked up, delivered, and cleaned up. Both the error-handling-to-filesystem handoff and the retry-detection-from-disk handoff are module boundaries that a unit test cannot verify. Real filesystem (temp directory) is necessary to prove state survives between invocations; real subprocess is not required since both runs can be driven by calling the entry-point function directly within one test process.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real temp directory as the output path, stub the email client to raise a connection error. Invoke the pipeline with a pre-built report payload. Assert the report is written to the expected file path. Then restore the email client stub to succeed and invoke the pipeline a second time. Assert the email client is called with the previously saved report content, the local file is removed after successful delivery, and no exception is raised.
